column1: Consolidated review: What does OptimizeWrite do in Delta Lake on Microsoft Fabric?
column2:
It reduces the number of files as data is written by producing fewer, larger files instead of many small files.

Why: this helps prevent the small-file problem and protects query performance.


Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/work-delta-lake-tables-fabric/3b-optimize-delta-tables

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Is OptimizeWrite enabled by default in Microsoft Fabric?
column2:
Yes. OptimizeWrite is enabled by default, and it can be enabled or disabled at the Spark session level.

Example config: spark.conf.set('spark.microsoft.delta.optimizeWrite.enabled', True).


Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/work-delta-lake-tables-fabric/3b-optimize-delta-tables

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What does the Delta table OPTIMIZE maintenance operation do?
column2:
It consolidates small Parquet files into fewer large files.

Expected benefits: fewer files, better compression, and more efficient data distribution across nodes.


Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/work-delta-lake-tables-fabric/3b-optimize-delta-tables

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Where can you run OPTIMIZE for a Delta table in a Fabric lakehouse UI?
column2:
In Lakehouse Explorer, open the table's menu, select Maintenance, choose Run OPTIMIZE command, optionally apply V-Order, then Run now.


Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/work-delta-lake-tables-fabric/3b-optimize-delta-tables

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What is V-Order in Microsoft Fabric?
column2:
V-Order is an optimization for Parquet files in Fabric that improves read speed and cost efficiency by optimizing layout/encoding/compression.

It is designed to make reads faster for Fabric compute engines such as Power BI, SQL, Spark, and others.


Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/work-delta-lake-tables-fabric/3b-optimize-delta-tables

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Is V-Order enabled by default in Microsoft Fabric, and what is the tradeoff?
column2:
Yes, V-Order is enabled by default and applied as data is written. It adds about 15% write overhead but improves read performance.


Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/work-delta-lake-tables-fabric/3b-optimize-delta-tables

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Which engines take full advantage of V-Order through Microsoft Verti-Scan technology?
column2:
Power BI and SQL engines.

Spark and other engines do not use Verti-Scan, but still benefit from V-Order optimization.


Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/work-delta-lake-tables-fabric/3b-optimize-delta-tables

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: When might disabling V-Order be beneficial?
column2:
For write-intensive scenarios such as staging data stores where data is only read once or twice.

Why: avoiding V-Order's write overhead may reduce total ingestion/processing time.


Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/work-delta-lake-tables-fabric/3b-optimize-delta-tables

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What does VACUUM do for a Delta table?
column2:
VACUUM permanently removes old Parquet data files that are no longer referenced by the transaction log and are older than the retention period.

It does not remove transaction logs.


Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/work-delta-lake-tables-fabric/3b-optimize-delta-tables

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: How does VACUUM affect Delta time travel?
column2:
After VACUUM removes old files, you cannot time travel back earlier than the retention period.


Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/work-delta-lake-tables-fabric/3b-optimize-delta-tables

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What is the default VACUUM retention period in Microsoft Fabric Delta tables?
column2:
7 days, or 168 hours. The system prevents using a shorter retention period.


Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/work-delta-lake-tables-fabric/3b-optimize-delta-tables

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: How can VACUUM be run in Microsoft Fabric?
column2:
Ad hoc through the lakehouse table maintenance UI, or scheduled using Fabric notebooks. It can also be run as a SQL command in a notebook.

Example: VACUUM lakehouse2.products RETAIN 168 HOURS;


Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/work-delta-lake-tables-fabric/3b-optimize-delta-tables

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: When should you use partitioning for Delta tables?
column2:
Use it for very large data volumes when the table can be split into a few large partitions that match common query filters.


Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/work-delta-lake-tables-fabric/3b-optimize-delta-tables

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: When should you avoid partitioning Delta tables?
column2:
Avoid it when data volumes are small, the partition column has high cardinality, or it would create multiple partition levels.

Why: this can create too many files/folders and worsen performance.


Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/work-delta-lake-tables-fabric/3b-optimize-delta-tables

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What PySpark pattern creates a partitioned Delta table by Category?
column2:
df.write.format('delta').partitionBy('Category').saveAsTable('partitioned_products', path='abfs_path/partitioned_products')


Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/work-delta-lake-tables-fabric/3b-optimize-delta-tables

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: A Fabric workspace contains a warehouse named Warehouse1. An on-premises SQL Server database is accessed through an on-premises data gateway. You need to copy data from the database to Warehouse1. Which item should you use?
column2:
Use a data pipeline.

Why: Data pipelines orchestrate Copy Data activities and are the right fit for copying data from an on-premises source into a Fabric warehouse.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What does IR mean in the context of Fabric/Data Factory pipelines?
column2:
IR means Integration Runtime.

It is the compute/infrastructure used by Data Factory to connect to data sources and run data movement/transformation activities.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Can a Fabric Data Factory pipeline run fail because an integration runtime reaches capacity limits?
column2:
Yes. A pipeline can fail if the integration runtime capacity is exhausted. Possible mitigations include scheduling runs at different times or distributing workloads across integration runtimes.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: How can partitioning tables in a Microsoft Fabric warehouse enhance performance?
column2:
By enabling parallel processing and efficient data pruning.

Why: queries can process relevant partitions and avoid scanning unnecessary data.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What type of Spark pool is a pre-hydrated live pool automatically created to start Apache Spark sessions faster in Fabric?
column2:
A starter pool.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Do scheduled pipelines in Microsoft Fabric only run once per day?
column2:
No. Scheduled pipelines are not limited to once per day; they can be scheduled at different supported frequencies.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Which monitoring tool provides visibility into Fabric capacity usage and helps optimize data warehouse performance?
column2:
The Microsoft Fabric Capacity Metrics app.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: For ad-hoc analysis on data within a Fabric warehouse, which option fits best: Dataflows Gen2, data pipelines, or a T-SQL notebook?
column2:
A T-SQL notebook.

Why: ad-hoc analysis in a warehouse is SQL-focused; pipelines/dataflows are more about data movement and transformation workflows.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What predefined classification can be applied to data within a Fabric domain for consistent protection and compliance?
column2:
Default sensitivity label.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What can resolve an 'Access token validation failed' error when accessing an API from a Fabric notebook?
column2:
Verify and update the access token's permissions, and ensure the token has not expired.

Why: the error often happens when the token lacks required permissions or is expired.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Can the Aggregate transformation in Fabric Data Factory be used to remove duplicate rows?
column2:
Yes, it can group by desired columns and use aggregate functions to retain the needed values.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Does Eventstream allow transformations and filters before routing data to final destinations?
column2:
Yes. Eventstream can apply transformations and filters before routing events to destinations.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Which Fabric role can only view content and cannot execute or cancel pipeline runs?
column2:
Viewer.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Which Fabric component provides built-in data visualization capabilities in the Spark learning context?
column2:
Notebook.

Why: Fabric notebooks can display results and create built-in charts from query/DataFrame outputs.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What does workspace.lakehouse.schema.table represent?
column2:
A fully qualified object path/name: workspace → lakehouse → schema → table.

It identifies exactly which table you are referencing when multiple workspaces, lakehouses, or schemas exist.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: When configuring a Data Factory pipeline to ingest data into a lakehouse, what setup is necessary?
column2:
Set up a Copy Data activity in the pipeline and specify the lakehouse as the destination.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What connection mode does Power BI use by default when connecting to a lakehouse semantic model?
column2:
Direct Lake mode.

Why: Direct Lake reads directly from Delta Lake files without importing/copying data into the Power BI model.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What does Delta Lake bring to Spark that resembles relational database transactional support?
column2:
ACID transaction support through a transaction log and serializable isolation for concurrent operations.

ACID = Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability.


Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/use-apache-spark-work-files-lakehouse/5-spark-sql

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What is a practical example of ACID atomicity in a Delta table?
column2:
If a job updates 10,000 rows but fails halfway, Delta should not leave the table half-updated. The transaction either commits as one unit or rolls back/does not become visible.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What is V-Order in plain terms?
column2:
A Fabric optimization for Delta-Parquet files that arranges and encodes data to make reads faster, especially for Power BI and SQL workloads.


Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/work-delta-lake-tables-fabric/3b-optimize-delta-tables

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Can you use Microsoft Fabric V-Order on a normal local Spark setup?
column2:
Not in the same Fabric-integrated way. V-Order is a Microsoft Fabric optimization. A local Spark setup can use standard Parquet/Delta optimizations, but not Fabric's full V-Order/Verti-Scan integration.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Which Fabric tool is best for low-code data cleaning?
column2:
Dataflow Gen2.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Which Fabric tool is best for orchestrating activities?
column2:
Data pipeline.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Which Fabric tool is best for complex PySpark transformations?
column2:
Notebook.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Which Fabric item is best for SQL analytics or dimensional modeling?
column2:
Warehouse.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Which Fabric item is best for batch files and Delta tables?
column2:
Lakehouse.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Which Fabric item is best for real-time telemetry or logs?
column2:
Eventhouse / KQL database.


Source link: https://microsoftlearning.github.io/mslearn-fabric/Instructions/Labs/12-query-data-in-kql-database.html

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Which Fabric feature should you use to avoid copying external data while still accessing it?
column2:
Shortcut.

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Which Fabric tool is best for streaming ingestion?
column2:
Eventstream.


Source link: https://microsoftlearning.github.io/mslearn-fabric/Instructions/Labs/12-query-data-in-kql-database.html

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What is the correct KQL way to select February rows from a Date column?
column2:
Use datetime_part('month', Date) == 2 in a where clause.

Example:
move_out
| where datetime_part('month', Date) == 2
| summarize Cost = sum(expenses) by items

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: In KQL, what does this query return: Bikestream | summarize ['Total Number of Bikes'] = sum(No_Bikes) by Neighbourhood | project Neighbourhood, ['Total Number of Bikes']?
column2:
One row per Neighbourhood with the summed number of bikes for that neighbourhood, showing only Neighbourhood and Total Number of Bikes.


Source link: https://microsoftlearning.github.io/mslearn-fabric/Instructions/Labs/12-query-data-in-kql-database.html

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What are materialized views?
column2:
Precomputed query results stored for faster reads. In KQL databases, they can speed up repeated aggregations over large or streaming datasets by maintaining precomputed aggregation results.


Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/query-data-kql-database-microsoft-fabric/6-knowledge-check

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Which security and governance topics does the Microsoft DP-700 study guide expect you to review?
column2:
Workspace-level and item-level access controls; row-level, column-level, object-level, folder-level, and file-level access controls; dynamic data masking; sensitivity labels; item endorsements; Microsoft Fabric audit logs; and OneLake security.

Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/credentials/certifications/resources/study-guides/dp-700

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What does dynamic data masking do in a Fabric Data Warehouse?
column2:
It limits sensitive data exposure by masking values in query results for nonprivileged users. The stored data is not changed; masking rules are applied when users query the warehouse or SQL analytics endpoint.

Local support: files/Dynamic data masking in Fabric Data Warehouse.pdf and files/24.pdf.

Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-warehouse/dynamic-data-masking

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Which dynamic data masking function should you use to reveal only selected characters of a string, such as the last four digits of a credit card number?
column2:
Use the custom string masking function partial(prefix, padding, suffix). For example, keeping only the last four characters uses a zero-character prefix, masking padding, and a four-character suffix.

Local support: files/24.pdf and files/30.pdf.

Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-warehouse/dynamic-data-masking

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Which permission lets a Fabric warehouse user view unmasked values from a dynamically masked column?
column2:
Grant UNMASK on the column, or grant CONTROL on the database. Users without the needed elevated permission see masked query results when selecting masked columns.

Local support: files/26.pdf.

Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-warehouse/dynamic-data-masking

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Why should dynamic data masking not be the only protection for sensitive warehouse data?
column2:
It can reduce accidental exposure, but users with query access may infer masked values through carefully crafted filters or repeated queries. Combine it with least privilege, object-level security, column-level security, and row-level security.

Source links: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-warehouse/dynamic-data-masking; https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-warehouse/security

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: When should you use column-level security in a Fabric warehouse?
column2:
Use column-level security when users can access a table but must not see specific sensitive columns, such as medical history, salary, social security numbers, transaction details, or credit card data.

Local support: files/25.pdf and files/27.pdf.

Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-warehouse/column-level-security

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: How do you implement column-level security in a Fabric warehouse?
column2:
Use T-SQL permissions such as GRANT SELECT ON TableName(AllowedColumn1, AllowedColumn2) for the allowed columns, preferably granting permissions to roles instead of individual users.

Local support: files/28.pdf.

Source links: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-warehouse/column-level-security; https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-warehouse/sql-granular-permissions

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What are SQL granular permissions used for in Microsoft Fabric warehouses?
column2:
They provide finer-grained access control when workspace roles or item permissions are too broad. Use standard T-SQL constructs such as GRANT, DENY, and REVOKE, and assign users to built-in or custom SQL roles.

Local support: files/SQL granular permissions in Microsoft Fabric.pdf and files/28.pdf.

Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-warehouse/sql-granular-permissions

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What minimum access does a user need before SQL granular permissions can let them connect to a Fabric warehouse or SQL analytics endpoint?
column2:
The user needs a workspace role or the item Read permission. Without at least Read access at the Fabric item level, the connection fails even if SQL permissions have been defined.

Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-warehouse/sql-granular-permissions

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: When should you use row-level security in a Fabric warehouse?
column2:
Use row-level security when users should only see authorized rows, such as their own department's rows or their tenant's data in a multitenant table.

Local support: files/29.pdf.

Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-warehouse/row-level-security

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What two database objects are central to a row-level security implementation in Fabric data warehousing?
column2:
An inline table-valued predicate function defines the allowed rows, and a security policy applies that predicate to a table. Filter predicates silently filter rows returned to read operations.

Local support: files/29.pdf.

Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-warehouse/row-level-security

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What is object-level security in a Fabric warehouse?
column2:
Object-level security controls access to database objects such as tables, views, or procedures based on user privileges or roles, so users can interact only with objects they have permission to use.

Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-warehouse/security

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Why prefer item permissions plus SQL granular permissions over broad workspace roles for a read-only warehouse user?
column2:
This follows least privilege. Workspace roles can grant broad access across items, while sharing the warehouse and then granting SQL permissions can limit the user to the required warehouse objects or columns.

Local support: files/Secure your Fabric Data Warehouse.pdf and files/Share your data and manage permissions.pdf.

Source links: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-warehouse/security; https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-warehouse/share-warehouse-manage-permissions

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: How should you share a Fabric warehouse when another user needs granular access to specific objects?
column2:
Share the warehouse without broad additional data permissions, then grant access to the specific objects with T-SQL permissions such as GRANT. This avoids giving unnecessary workspace-wide or warehouse-wide access.

Local support: files/Share your data and manage permissions.pdf.

Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-warehouse/share-warehouse-manage-permissions

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What can Microsoft Fabric audit logs help you answer for a warehouse or SQL analytics endpoint?
column2:
They help identify who took what action on Fabric items, supporting compliance, records management, activity review, and security investigations.

Source links: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-warehouse/security; https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/admin/track-user-activities

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What does endorsement support in Microsoft Fabric governance?
column2:
Endorsement helps users find trusted, reusable content and data by labeling items with badges such as Promoted, Certified, or Master data. Certified items indicate organization-approved quality and reliability.

Source links: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/governance/endorsement-overview; https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/credentials/certifications/resources/study-guides/dp-700

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: What is the role of sensitivity labels in DP-700 security and governance?
column2:
Sensitivity labels classify and protect Fabric items according to organizational data protection rules, helping apply consistent handling for confidential or regulated data.

Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/credentials/certifications/resources/study-guides/dp-700

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: Before visualizing Eventhouse data in a Real-Time Dashboard, what must the dashboard tile have?
column2:
A tile needs an underlying KQL query and a connected data source such as an Eventhouse or KQL database. A KQL queryset can be used to write the query and save it to a dashboard; this is different from enabling a tenant-level visualization feature.

Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/real-time-intelligence/dashboard-real-time-create

Local support: files/dp700_realtime_intelligence_wrong_answers.csv

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: When streaming transaction events need anomaly conditions and automated actions, why choose Activator instead of a Real-Time Dashboard?
column2:
Activator rules continuously evaluate event or object-property conditions and can trigger actions such as Teams notifications, Fabric pipelines, notebooks, Spark jobs, functions, or Power Automate flows. A Real-Time Dashboard is mainly for querying and visualizing results.

Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/real-time-intelligence/data-activator/activator-rules-overview

Local support: files/dp700_realtime_intelligence_wrong_answers.csv

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: When combining temperature and humidity streams in Eventstreams, when is join logic a better fit than union?
column2:
Use join logic when related events from two streams must be correlated by a key or matching condition, such as device and time. Union-style logic appends events into one stream; it does not match related records from separate streams.

Source links: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/real-time-intelligence/event-streams/overview; https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/real-time-intelligence/event-streams/process-events-using-event-processor-editor

Local support: files/dp700_realtime_intelligence_wrong_answers.csv

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt

column1: Consolidated review: For delays in real-time IoT ingestion and transformation, which Fabric item should you inspect first?
column2:
Inspect the Eventstream, because eventstreams bring real-time events into Fabric, optionally transform them, and route them to destinations such as Eventhouse, KQL database, Lakehouse, custom endpoints, or Activator. Real-Time Hub is not the component that performs the ingestion and transformation pipeline.

Source link: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/real-time-intelligence/event-streams/overview

Local support: files/dp700_realtime_intelligence_wrong_answers.csv

Source file: files/DP-700 Consolidated Review.txt
